text: I have to believe that when things are bad I can change them.
context: The quiet, powerful belief that fuels his comeback from rock bottom during the Great Depression.

text: You think you're telling me something? Like, what, boxing is dangerous? You don't think working triple shifts and at night on a scaffold isn't just as likely to get a man killed?
context: Defending his right to fight to feed his family when the 'safe' jobs are just as deadly.

text: I believe we live in a great country, a country that's great enough to help a man financially when he's in trouble.
context: Humble acceptance of help while maintaining dignity and the drive to repay it.

text: You're everybody's hope, and the kids' hero, and you are the champion of my heart, James J. Braddock.
context: His wife reminding him what he is fighting for beyond the ring.

text: I know what I'm fighting for.
context: The simple, profound answer: fighting for his family gives him strength no training can match.

text: I have to believe that when things are bad I can change them.
context: Repeated as the core philosophy that keeps him swinging when everything is against him.

text: Whatever Braddock was gonna do in boxing, I guess he's done it.
context: The world writing him off, which only fuels his legendary return.

text: He gave his kids everything he had. He fought for them.
context: The ultimate measure of the man: a fighter whose greatest victories were for his family.
